description: Seventh curated wave focused on repeated dev-check review cases with explicit client-side metadata or file-name fallbacks.

rules.reason: Fabric metadata explicitly marks BHMenu as environment=client and only exposes multiplayer-menu entrypoints.
rules.notes: Fills the remaining Fabric 2.4.1 version gap so malformed jars from Better MC stop falling through review.

rules.reason: NeoForge metadata sets displayTest=NONE and marks its runtime dependencies as CLIENT-only.
rules.notes: Promoted from repeated FTB StoneBlock 4 review hits after checking the bundled neoforge.mods.toml descriptors.

rules.reason: NeoForge metadata only requires lambdynlights_runtime on the CLIENT side, matching the Fabric client-only environment signal.
rules.notes: Extends the earlier Fabric-only LambDynamicLights rule to the NeoForge bundle variant seen in Medieval MC.

rules.reason: Fabric metadata explicitly marks the mod as client-only, and repeated runtime review only exposes renderer and dynamic-lighting integrations.
rules.notes: Promoted to cover the cross-platform bundled jar variant that still falls through review in Better MC.

rules.reason: Metadata consistently marks the mod as client-only and its bundled runtime depends on Sodium Options API only on the CLIENT side.
rules.notes: Promoted from repeated Better MC and Medieval MC review cases after checking fabric.mod.json and bundled NeoForge descriptors.
